specification.topic-statement: The provider factory validates a provider name, resolves its API credential, and returns an initialized LLM provider instance ready for use.

scope.in-scope: Provider name validation, credential resolution, dispatch to provider-specific constructors, per-agent instance caching, and cache invalidation.
scope.boundary:
Credential resolution delegates to the credential loading subsystem with a null path (triggering default path discovery). This specification records what credential name is requested and what value is received — the credential loading internals are out of scope.
Credentials are loaded from `~/.config/ikigai/credentials.json` (or `$IKIGAI_CONFIG_DIR/credentials.json` if that variable is set), with environment variables applied as overrides. Only non-empty string values are accepted from either source. A missing file is not an error. JSON parse failures are silently ignored; in that case only environment variables contribute.

After credential validation, an OpenAI-, Anthropic-, or Google-specific constructor is called with the resolved API key. Whether that constructor succeeds, and what it allocates internally, is out of scope.

field.name: name
field.note: one of exactly three values: 'openai', 'anthropic', 'google'; comparison is case-sensitive

behavior.provider-creation:
A provider instance is created by supplying a provider name string. The following steps execute in strict order; the first failure stops processing and returns an error.
The name is checked against the list of three supported provider names: `openai`, `anthropic`, and `google`. Comparison is case-sensitive. If the name does not match any of the three, an invalid-argument error is returned immediately. No allocations have been made at this point, so no cleanup is required.
`OpenAI`, `ANTHROPIC`, and `Google` are not valid provider names. Only lowercase forms are accepted.
The credential loading subsystem is invoked to load all credentials. If loading fails (for example, because `HOME` is not set and the default path requires tilde expansion), that error is returned immediately.
The provider name is mapped to its corresponding environment variable name, and the credential value for that name is retrieved from the loaded credentials. If the value is absent (neither the file nor the environment variable provided a non-empty value for this provider), an error is returned with a message naming the environment variable and the credentials file that should be used to supply the key.
Credential values are sourced first from the JSON configuration file and then overridden by environment variables. Empty environment variable values are treated as absent and do not override file values. Only non-empty string values from either source are accepted.
The resolved API key is passed to the provider-specific constructor for the validated provider name. If that constructor fails, its error is returned to the caller. If it succeeds, the resulting provider instance is returned.
behavior.per-agent-caching:
Each agent holds at most one provider instance. The instance is created on the first request and cached for all subsequent requests within that agent's lifetime.
When a provider instance is first requested for an agent, the agent's configured provider name is passed to the provider creation procedure above. If the agent has no provider configured, an error is returned. On success, the resulting instance is stored in the agent and returned.
When a provider instance is requested and one is already cached, the cached instance is returned immediately. No credential loading, no name validation, and no provider construction occurs.
Each agent creates its own independent provider instance. Two agents configured with the same provider name each hold separate instances with separate internal state and separate HTTP sessions.
behavior.cache-invalidation:
The cached provider instance can be explicitly released. When released, the instance is freed and the cache slot is cleared. The next request for a provider instance triggers the full creation procedure again, including credential loading.
Releasing the cache when no instance is cached is a no-op.
Invalidation is triggered when the agent's provider or model configuration changes at runtime, ensuring the next request creates a fresh instance with the new configuration.
behavior.provider-enumeration: The list of supported provider names can be retrieved as a null-terminated array of strings. The array is static and its contents never change at runtime.
